name: Commerce.js
what_it_does: Headless e-commerce API and JavaScript SDK for building fully custom storefronts, providing programmatic management of products, carts, checkouts, orders, customers, and discounts.
use_cases: Building custom JAMstack storefronts (Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby) with complete control over the shopping UI
Agents managing product catalogs: creating, updating, and pricing products programmatically
Automating order management workflows: fulfillment status updates, refunds, and customer notifications
Building multi-channel commerce where the same product data powers web, mobile, and voice interfaces
not_for: Merchants who need an all-in-one platform with built-in storefront, hosting, and marketing tools (use Shopify instead)
Non-technical teams without frontend development capacity to build the storefront layer
High-volume enterprise commerce with complex B2B pricing rules, ERP integration, or multi-warehouse inventory
best_when: Best when a development team wants complete control over the customer-facing storefront experience and needs a clean API to decouple commerce logic from presentation, especially in JAMstack or headless architectures.
avoid_when: Avoid when speed-to-market is the top priority and you don't have frontend engineering resources, or when you need mature ecosystem plugins (shipping calculators, tax engines, loyalty programs).

auth.notes: Two key types: public key for client-side (storefront) operations like browsing products and creating carts; secret key for server-side operations like order management, customer data, and analytics. Public key is safe to expose in frontend code. Keys generated in the Chec dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier allows up to 10 products and 2 product variants with full API access.
pricing.paid_tiers: Entrepreneur plan at $79/month for unlimited products, more variants, and advanced features. Agency and Enterprise plans available with custom pricing for multi-store management.
pricing.notes: Payment processing fees are handled by the connected payment gateway (e.g., Stripe) and are not charged by Commerce.js directly. Chec does not take a transaction percentage.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits are not publicly documented in specific numbers. The JavaScript SDK includes built-in request handling. Rate limiting is enforced per API key.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON error objects with error type and message. Validation errors include field-level details. SDK wraps errors in consistent error objects. Overall error clarity is reasonable for a headless platform.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys for cart or order creation. Agents should check for existing carts/orders before creating new ones to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Cart tokens are ephemeral and expire — agents must persist cart IDs or recreate carts when tokens expire
Checkout tokens are single-use and short-lived (different from cart tokens) — must be generated immediately before payment capture
Variant and option IDs must be pre-fetched; there is no name-based lookup in the checkout API
Webhook payloads require signature verification using a shared secret — agents must implement HMAC validation
Public vs secret key distinction means client-side and server-side operations require different credentials to be available in different contexts

security_score_components.security_notes: Public/secret key separation is a good pattern for headless commerce — client-safe public key limits exposure. PCI-DSS compliance handled through payment gateway (Stripe). No OAuth for third-party app delegation, which limits multi-tenant agent scenarios.
